concerns.text: The 'Assumptions' section contains a contradictory and malformed statement: 'The Open Science Framework MFQ dataset contains the required MFQ subscale scores **The OSF MFQ dataset does not include Morningness-Eveningness Questionnaire (MEQ) scores...**'. This appears to be a copy-paste error where a correction note was merged into the assumption text, creating a logical contradiction that obscures the data source strategy.

FR-003 mandates a 'one-way ANOVA' with 'chronotype as the factor and PSQI, age, and sex as covariates'. This is a methodological contradiction. A one-way ANOVA does not include covariates; a model with covariates is an ANCOVA (Analysis of Covariance). The spec must clarify the statistical model type to ensure the implementation matches the research intent of 'controlling for sleep quality and demographics'.

SC-001 requires 'Chronotype classification accuracy is ≥ 95% when compared to a manually coded benchmark dataset'. The idea and spec do not define a 'manually coded benchmark dataset' or a ground truth for chronotype (which is inherently subjective). This success criterion is untestable without an external gold standard that is not described in the scope.
